folio.label: 184r
folio.l: Were no dispite I don , for his deffame
Hi~ self he slow , he coude no bettre red
Of whicú , fortune lougú , & madehaddehaddemade a game
¢ Was neuäe capitaigne , vnder a kyngö
That Regnes moo , putte in subieccou~
Ne strengere was in feld , of alle thyngö
As in his tyme , ne grettere of renoun~
Ne more pompous , in heigú päsumpciou~
Than Olyferne , whicú fortune ay kiste
So lykerously , & ladde hi~ vp & doun
Til that his hed was of , or that he wiste
¢ Nat oonly æ this world , had hi~ in awe
For lesynge of richesse , or libertee
But made euäy man , reneye his lawe
Nabugodonosor , was god , seide he
Noon other god , shulde adhoured be
Ageyns this hest , no wygút dar trespace
Saue in Bethulya , a strongö Citee
Where Eliachym , a Preest was , of æ place
¢ But take kepe of the detú , of Oliferne
Amydde his oost , he dronke lay a nygút
W Inne his tent , large as is a berne
And ©et for al his pompe , & al his mygút
Iuditú a wo~man , as he lay vp rigút
Slepynge , his hed of smote , & from his tente
Ful pryuyly she stal , from euäy wygút
And witú his hed , vn to hire toun she wente
¢ What nedetú it , of kyngö Anthiocus
To telle , his heygú , real mageste
His heigú pride , his werkes venimë
For swicú a nother was æäe noon , as he
Rede whicú that he was , in Machabe
And rede the proude , that he seide
And why he fel , fro heigú ¸sperite
And in an hil , how wrecchedly he deyde
¢ Fortune hi~ hadde , enhaunced so in pride
That verreyly , he wende he mygúte atteyne
Vn to the sterres , vp on euäy syde
And in balaunce weyghen , ecú mounteigne
And alle the floodes , of the see , restreyne
And goddes peeple , had he moost in hate
Hem wolde he sleen , in tåment and in peyne
Wenyngö æ god ne mygúte , his péde abate
¢ And for æ Nichanor , and Thimothe
